_meta.note: Phase 56 Plan 05 AI first-pass translation. Steuerberater review is a Phase 56 gate owned by Plan 08. Strings without dictionary coverage remain in English fallback and require Steuerberater translation pass.

approvals.heading: Approval chains
approvals.description: Configure approval chains for invoices. Chains are matched based on conditions like amount thresholds.

empty.heading: No approval chains configured
empty.body: Create an approval chain to route invoices through multi-level approvals before payment.

deleteConfirm.body: This chain will be permanently deleted. In-flight approvals using this chain will not be affected.

editor.editDescription: Update chain settings, approval levels, and routing conditions.
editor.defaultHelp: The default chain is used when no conditions match.
editor.conditionsHelp: When an invoice matches these conditions, this chain is used. Leave empty for the default chain.

notFound.heading: Seite nicht gefunden
notFound.body: Die gesuchte Seite existiert nicht oder wurde verschoben.

serverError.heading: Etwas ist schiefgelaufen
serverError.body: Auf unserer Seite ist ein unerwarteter Fehler aufgetreten. Versuchen Sie es erneut oder kontaktieren Sie den Support, falls das Problem weiterhin besteht.

Errors.UNAUTHORIZED: Sie sind nicht berechtigt, diese Aktion auszuführen.
Errors.ACCOUNT_BANNED: Your account has been deactivated. Please contact your organization administrator.
Errors.ACCOUNT_LOCKED: Too many failed login attempts. Your account is temporarily locked. Please try again later.
Errors.LAST_ADMIN_CANNOT_DEACTIVATE: Cannot deactivate the last administrator. Assign another admin first.
Errors.PERMISSION_DENIED: Sie haben keine Berechtigung, diese Aktion auszuführen.
Errors.UNKNOWN_ERROR: Ein unerwarteter Fehler ist aufgetreten. Versuchen Sie es erneut.
Errors.CONTRACTOR_HAS_UNPAID_INVOICES: Cannot archive contractor with unpaid invoices. Resolve all pending payments first.
Errors.CONTRACTOR_HAS_ACTIVE_WORKFLOWS: Cannot archive contractor with active workflows. Complete or cancel workflows first.
Errors.CONTRACTOR_HAS_ACTIVE_CONTRACTS: Cannot archive contractor with active contracts. End or reassign contracts first.
Errors.CONTRACTOR_INVALID_TRANSITION: This status transition is not allowed.
Errors.CONTRACT_END_DATE_BEFORE_START: End date must be after start date.
Errors.CONTRACT_INVALID_TRANSITION: This status transition is not allowed.
Errors.INVOICE_NOT_RECEIVED_STATUS: Invoice must be in RECEIVED status to submit for matching.
Errors.INVOICE_CONTRACTOR_NOT_FOUND: Contractor not found in this organization.
Errors.INVOICE_CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND: Contract not found in this organization.
Errors.INVOICE_DUPLICATE: A duplicate invoice already exists with the same number, seller, and amount.
Errors.INVOICE_AMOUNT_MISMATCH: The invoice amount does not match the expected value for this line item.
Errors.PAYMENT_INVOICES_NOT_READY: Some invoices are not ready for payment.
Errors.PAYMENT_INVOICES_NOT_FOUND: One or more invoices not found in this organization.
Errors.PAYMENT_RUN_INVALID_STATUS: This action is not allowed for the current payment run status.
Errors.PAYMENT_RUN_NOT_DRAFT: Can only remove invoices from draft payment runs.
Errors.PAYMENT_INVOICE_NOT_IN_RUN: Invoice not found in this payment run.
Errors.PAYMENT_MIXED_CURRENCIES: All invoices in a payment run must use the same currency.
Errors.APPROVAL_CHAIN_HAS_ACTIVE_FLOWS: Cannot delete chain with active approval flows.
Errors.APPROVAL_NOT_ASSIGNED: You are not the assigned approver for this step.
Errors.APPROVAL_SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN: You cannot approve your own submission.
Errors.APPROVAL_DELEGATE_NOT_MEMBER: Delegate user is not a member of this organization.
Errors.WORKFLOW_RUN_ALREADY_CANCELLED: Workflow run has already been cancelled.
Errors.WORKFLOW_TASK_INVALID_STATUS: This status transition is not allowed for this task.
Errors.WORKFLOW_TASK_CANNOT_SKIP: This task cannot be skipped in its current status.
Errors.DOCUMENT_FILE_TYPE_NOT_ALLOWED: File type is not allowed. Accepted: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PNG, JPG.
Errors.DOCUMENT_NOT_IN_STORAGE: Uploaded file not found in storage. Please try uploading again.
Errors.DOCUMENT_INFECTED: This document has been flagged as infected and cannot be downloaded.
Errors.DOCUMENT_NOT_ACTIVE: Only active documents can be versioned.
Errors.PORTAL_PENDING_CHANGE_EXISTS: You already have a pending change request.
Errors.PORTAL_BILLING_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND: Default billing profile not found for contractor.
Errors.INTEGRATION_NO_OAUTH: This provider does not support OAuth.
Errors.SETTINGS_SUBDOMAIN_TAKEN: This subdomain is already in use.
Errors.ESIGN_DOWNLOAD_FAILED: Aktion fehlgeschlagen: download document from storage.
Errors.VALIDATION_COUNTRY_CODE_LENGTH: Country code must be exactly 2 characters.
Errors.VALIDATION_TAX_ID_FK_REQUIRED: Contractor Tax ID is required for linking.
Errors.VALIDATION_FAILURE_REASON_REQUIRED: Failure reason required when marking as failed.

introduction.heading: 1. Introduction
introduction.body: Contractor Ops ("we", "us", "our") operates the contractorops.com platform. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal data when you use our services. We are committed to protecting your privacy in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the Polish Act on Personal Data Protection, and other applicable data protection laws.

dataCollected.heading: 2. Data We Collect
dataCollected.body: We collect the following categories of personal data: Account data (name, email address, organization name); Contractor data (legal name, tax identification numbers (NIP), bank account details, addresses, phone numbers, email addresses); Invoice and payment data (invoice numbers, amounts, payment details); Usage data (IP addresses, browser type, access times, pages visited); Integration data (tokens and identifiers for connected services such as Slack, Jira, Google Calendar).

purpose.heading: 3. How We Use Your Data
purpose.body: We process personal data for the following purposes: Providing and maintaining our contractor management platform; Processing invoices and facilitating payment workflows; Managing contractor onboarding and offboarding workflows; Sending transactional notifications (approval requests, invoice alerts, contract reminders); Ensuring security and preventing fraud; Complying with legal obligations (tax reporting, audit requirements).

legalBasis.heading: 4. Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.body: We process your data based on: Performance of a contract (providing our services to you); Legitimate interests (improving our platform, ensuring security); Legal obligations (tax compliance, financial record-keeping); Consent (for optional features like analytics and marketing communications, which you can withdraw at any time).

dataSharing.heading: 5. Data Sharing and Sub-processors
dataSharing.body: We share data with the following categories of third parties only as necessary to provide our services: Cloud infrastructure providers (Vercel, Neon, Cloudflare) for hosting and data storage; Payment processors (Stripe) for subscription billing; Email service providers (Resend) for transactional notifications; Error monitoring (Sentry) for application reliability; Log aggregation (Axiom) for operational monitoring. We do not sell your personal data. All sub-processors are bound by data processing agreements ensuring GDPR compliance.

retention.heading: 6. Data Retention
retention.body: We retain your personal data for the duration of your subscription plus 30 days. Financial records (invoices, payment data) are retained for the legally required period (typically 5-10 years depending on jurisdiction). You may request earlier deletion of non-legally-required data at any time. Deleted data is soft-deleted immediately and permanently purged within 90 days.

security.heading: 7. Security Measures
security.body: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data, including: Encryption at rest (database-level and application-level for sensitive fields such as bank accounts); Encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+ for all connections); Role-based access control with principle of least privilege; Audit logging of all access to sensitive data; Regular security assessments; Virus scanning of uploaded documents.

rights.heading: 8. Your Rights
rights.body: Under GDPR, you have the right to: Access your personal data; Rectify inaccurate data; Request erasure of your data (right to be forgotten); Restrict processing of your data; Data portability (receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format); Object to processing based on legitimate interests; Withdraw consent at any time. To exercise these rights, contact us at privacy@contractorops.com. We will respond within 30 days.

cookies.heading: 9. Cookies
cookies.body: We use strictly necessary cookies for authentication and session management. These cookies are essential for the platform to function and cannot be disabled. We do not use third-party tracking cookies or advertising cookies. Session cookies expire after 24 hours of inactivity.

contact.heading: 10. Contact
contact.body: For privacy-related inquiries, contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@contractorops.com. If you believe your data protection rights have been violated, you have the right to lodge a complaint with your local supervisory authority. In Poland, this is the President of the Personal Data Protection Office (UODO).

acceptance.heading: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.body: By accessing or using Contractor Ops ("the Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you are using the Service on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have the authority to bind that organization to these terms. If you do not agree, you may not use the Service.

serviceDescription.heading: 2. Service Description
serviceDescription.body: Contractor Ops is a SaaS platform for managing contractor relationships, including onboarding, contract management, invoice processing, approval workflows, and payment facilitation. The Service is provided on an as-is basis. We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue features with reasonable notice.

accounts.heading: 3. Accounts and Access
accounts.body: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials. You must notify us immediately of any unauthorized access. Each user account is for a single individual and may not be shared. Organization administrators are responsible for managing user access and permissions within their organization.

dataProcessing.heading: 4. Data Processing
dataProcessing.body: You act as the Data Controller for contractor data processed through our platform. We act as the Data Processor. Our processing activities are governed by our Data Processing Agreement (DPA), available upon request. You are responsible for ensuring you have a lawful basis for processing contractor personal data through our platform.

acceptableUse.heading: 5. Acceptable Use
acceptableUse.body: You agree not to: Use the Service for any unlawful purpose; Upload malicious files or attempt to compromise the platform's security; Attempt to access data belonging to other organizations; Exceed reasonable usage limits or abuse API endpoints; Reverse-engineer, decompile, or attempt to extract the source code of the Service.

intellectualProperty.heading: 6. Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.body: The Service, including its code, design, and documentation, is owned by Contractor Ops. You retain ownership of all data you upload to the Service. We claim no intellectual property rights over your contractor data, invoices, or documents.

liability.heading: 7. Limitation of Liability
liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Contractor Ops shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, or business opportunities. Our total liability shall not exceed the amount paid by you in the 12 months preceding the claim. This limitation does not apply to liability that cannot be excluded by law.

termination.heading: 8. Termination
termination.body: Either party may terminate this agreement at any time. Upon termination: Your access to the Service will be revoked; Your data will be retained for 30 days, after which it will be permanently deleted; You may request a data export before termination. We may suspend or terminate your account immediately if you violate these terms.

governingLaw.heading: 9. Governing Law
governingLaw.body: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Poland. Any disputes arising from these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in Warsaw, Poland. For EU consumers, this does not affect your right to bring proceedings in your country of residence.

contact.heading: 10. Contact
contact.body: For questions about these Terms, contact us at legal@contractorops.com.

introduction.heading: Übersicht
introduction.body: Contractor Ops uses the following third-party sub-processors to deliver our services. Each sub-processor is bound by a data processing agreement ensuring GDPR compliance. We only share the minimum data necessary for each processor to fulfil its function.

changes.heading: Changes to Sub-processors
changes.body: We will notify customers at least 30 days in advance of any changes to this sub-processor list. Notifications will be sent via email to organization administrators. If you object to a new sub-processor, you may terminate your subscription before the change takes effect.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.body: For questions about our sub-processors or data processing practices, contact us at privacy@contractorops.com.

introduction.heading: 1. Purpose
introduction.body: This document describes Contractor Ops' procedure for detecting, assessing, and notifying relevant parties in the event of a personal data breach, in accordance with Articles 33 and 34 of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

definition.heading: 2. What Constitutes a Data Breach
definition.body: A personal data breach is a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to, personal data transmitted, stored, or otherwise processed. This includes: unauthorized access to user accounts or organization data; accidental exposure of personal data to unintended recipients; loss or theft of data storage devices or backups; malicious attacks resulting in data exfiltration; accidental deletion of data without backup recovery capability.

detection.heading: 3. Detection and Reporting
detection.body: We employ multiple layers of monitoring to detect potential breaches: automated security monitoring via Sentry (error tracking and anomaly detection); structured logging via Axiom (access pattern analysis); audit logs capturing all access to sensitive data; external uptime monitoring via UptimeRobot; background job health monitoring for webhook processing integrity. Any team member who suspects a data breach must immediately report it to the security team at security@contractorops.com.

assessment.heading: 4. Breach Assessment
assessment.body: Upon detection, our incident response team will: confirm whether a breach has occurred; identify the scope and nature of data affected; assess the risk to individuals' rights and freedoms; determine the cause and implement immediate containment measures; classify the severity (low, medium, high, critical) based on data sensitivity, number of affected individuals, and potential for harm.

authorityNotification.heading: 5. Notification to Supervisory Authority
authorityNotification.body: If the breach is likely to result in a risk to individuals' rights and freedoms, we will notify the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach. For customers in Poland, this is the President of the Personal Data Protection Office (UODO). The notification will include: the nature of the breach and approximate number of affected individuals; the categories of data concerned; the likely consequences; the measures taken or proposed to address the breach.

customerNotification.heading: 6. Notification to Affected Customers
customerNotification.body: We will notify affected customers (data controllers) without undue delay via: email to all organization administrators; in-app notification banner for affected organizations; dedicated status page update. The notification will describe: what happened and when; what data was affected; what we have done to contain the breach; what steps customers should take; contact information for our security team. If the breach is likely to result in a high risk to individuals, we will also assist customers in notifying affected data subjects.

timeline.heading: 7. Response Timeline
timeline.body: Our target response timeline is: Immediate (0-1 hours): Containment and initial assessment; Within 4 hours: Internal incident team assembled, scope determined; Within 24 hours: Root cause identified, remediation plan in place; Within 72 hours: Supervisory authority notified (if required); Within 7 days: Full incident report provided to affected customers; Within 30 days: Post-incident review completed and preventive measures implemented.

documentation.heading: 8. Record Keeping
documentation.body: We maintain records of all data breaches, including those that do not require notification, documenting: the facts surrounding the breach; its effects; the remediation actions taken; the reasoning for decisions made regarding notification. These records are retained for a minimum of 5 years and are available for inspection by supervisory authorities upon request.

contact.heading: 9. Contact
contact.body: To report a suspected data breach or for questions about this procedure, contact our security team at security@contractorops.com. For general privacy inquiries, contact privacy@contractorops.com.

credits.exhausted: No credits remaining -- purchase more to continue OCR processing

topUp.title: Buy OCR Credits
topUp.description: Select a credit bundle. You will be redirected to Stripe to complete the purchase.
topUp.priceNote: Exact price will be confirmed on the Stripe checkout page.

errors.checkoutFailed: Aktion fehlgeschlagen: start checkout. Please try again.

overlay.paymentFailedBody: Update your payment method to avoid service interruption.

emptyStates.noPendingReviewsBody: All timesheets have been reviewed. Check back when contractors submit new entries.
emptyStates.noTimeEntriesBody: Time entries will appear here once contractors start logging hours.

detail.notFoundBody: The requested timesheet could not be found. It may have been removed or the URL is incorrect.

csrGeneration.title: Step 2 of 5: Generate Certificate Request
csrGeneration.description: A Certificate Signing Request will be generated using your tax details from Step 1.
csrGeneration.privateKeyNote: The private key will be stored securely in your organization's secret vault. It never leaves the server.

complianceChecks.title: Step 4 of 5: Run Compliance Checks
complianceChecks.description: 6 test invoices will be submitted to ZATCA's sandbox to verify your setup.

toast.allPassed: All 6 compliance checks passed. Your setup is ready for production.
toast.someFailed: Compliance check failed: {failedCount} test(s) did not pass. Review your tax details and try again.

statusCard.title: Peppol Network
statusCard.connectDescription: Connect to the Peppol network to send and receive e-invoices with UAE trading partners. You will need your TRN and ASP credentials.
statusCard.disconnectDescription: Your Participant ID will be deregistered and you will not be able to send or receive Peppol invoices. Continue?

taskConfig.configSaveFailed: Aktion fehlgeschlagen: save Jira task configuration.
taskConfig.enableToggle: Create Jira issue when task activates

statusMapping.title: Status Mapping
statusMapping.description: Map workflow task statuses to Jira transitions for {projectName}.
statusMapping.descriptionDefault: Map workflow task statuses to Jira transitions.
statusMapping.unmappedTooltip: Not mapped -- status changes for this state will be ignored

taskConfig.configSaveFailed: Aktion fehlgeschlagen: save Linear task configuration.

toast.saveFailed: Aktion fehlgeschlagen: save status mapping. Please try again.

returnApproved.title: Return request approved
returnApproved.body: Your return request has been approved. A shipping label has been generated.

returnRequested.title: New equipment return request
returnRequested.body: A contractor requested an equipment return.

tenant.noActiveOrganization: No active organization. Please select an organization first.

upload.rateLimitExceeded: Upload rate limit exceeded. Maximum 10 uploads per minute. Try again shortly.

zatca.apiClientUnavailable: ZATCA API client not available. Ensure e-invoicing engine is configured.
zatca.taxDetailsRequired: Tax details must be saved before generating CSR.
zatca.csrRequired: CSR must be generated before requesting compliance CSID.
zatca.taxDetailsRequiredForCompliance: Tax details required for compliance checks.
zatca.complianceCsidRequired: Compliance CSID must be obtained before running compliance checks.
zatca.complianceChecksMustPass: Compliance checks must pass before requesting production certificate.

timesheet.weekStartDateMustBeMonday: Week start date must be a Monday.
timesheet.canOnlyEditDraftOrRejected: Can only edit DRAFT or REJECTED timesheets.
timesheet.cannotSubmit: Timesheet cannot be submitted (wrong status or not found).
timesheet.cannotApprove: Timesheet cannot be approved (must be SUBMITTED).
timesheet.cannotReject: Timesheet cannot be rejected (must be SUBMITTED).

approval.noUserWithRole: No user with the required role found.
